checklist.criterion: Applies the failure-focused test to each Must item (explicit statement that release fails without it).
Must-tier effort is at or below 60% of total capacity (4w × 2 engineers = 8 engineer-weeks).
At least one item is classified as Won't with an explicit revisit date.
Output is saved to moscow-table.md as instructed.
Each item includes a business rationale in the table.
Document opens with a constraint summary (timeline, team size, scope-lock statement).
Table contains the required columns: Requirement | Category | Business Rationale | Risk if Omitted.
No item is labelled Must purely due to stakeholder preference; every Must passes the failure test.
Release constraints (date, staffing) are confirmed before categories are assigned.
All 8 backlog items are assigned a category (none omitted or left uncategorized).
